This is an alias for connection.channel()
One can redefine this for debugging purposes or to implement another protocol driver

:returns: pika.channel.Channel or compatible
:raises: Anything pika.channel.Channel can raise

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.test.synchron.mocks.queue_base.QueueBase.basic_args
documentation:
This is intended as an aid for argparse-driven commandline utilities
You may want to extend this method to add your own command-line arguments

```
param parser

Supply your own parser. If not specified - self.parser will be used

returns

Parser extended with RabbitMQ connection parameters
```

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.test.synchron.mocks.queue_base.QueueBase.setup_from_args
documentation:
This is an aid for your argparse-driven commandline application.
Converts argparse resulting namespace to ConnectionParameters you got
by parsing arguments using basic_args()-extended parser to connection
parameters.

```
param args

Argparse namespace, self.args used if not specified

returns

args used for setup

raises

See setup() method
```

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.test.synchron.mocks.queue_base.QueueBase.setup
documentation:
Sets up basic AMQP parameters. Call this (or setup_from_args()) before connecting

```
param url

AMQP url with or without username

param username

If supplied - overrides one specified in URL

param password

If supplied - overrides one specified in URL

param queue

Queue name to use

param declare

If set to True - will declare this queue on server

raises

Raises anything URLParameters() constructor can rise
```

This is a base class for RabbitMQ interactions. You probably do not want to use it directly.
Connection and channel setup is handled here (non-specific for client or server role).
It also meant to be integrated to argparse, if needed - see child classes for details

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.test.synchron.mocks.queue_application.QueueApplication.main
documentation:
Run this as your application's main() function

```
param cmdline

Command line parameters array for debug purposes

returns

return code for os.exit. Quit your application with this code
```

This class is intended for creating queue consumer application from scratch.
It takes everything needed including command line arguments processing except actual queue processing
that must be done by your own methods supplied by subclassing this class

documentation:
This is intended as an aid for argparse-driven commandline utilities

```
param parser

Supply your own parser. If not specified - self.parser will be used

returns

Parser extended with RabbitMQ connection parameters
```

documentation:
This is an aid for your argparse-driven commandline application.
Converts argparse resulting namespace to ConnectionParameters you got
by parsing arguments using basic_args()-extended parser to connection
parameters.

```
param args

Argparse namespace, self.args used if not specified

returns

args used for setup

raises

See setup() method
```

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_base.QueueBase.setup
documentation:
Sets up basic AMQP parameters. Call this (or setup_from_args()) before connecting

```
param url

AMQP url with or without username

param username

If supplied - overrides one specified in URL

param password

If supplied - overrides one specified in URL

param queue

Queue name to use

param queue_declare

['yes', 'no', 'only'], 'yes' - declare and continue, 'only' - declare and stop processing (for server', 'no' - do not declare

raises

Raises anything URLParameters() constructor can rise
```

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_base.QueueBase.connect
documentation:
Connects to server and allocates channel. If allready connected - replaces the old connection
with new one. Sets self.connection and seld.channel properties and declares queue if asked by setup()

To be implemented in derived classes

documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

classes.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_client.QueueClient

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_client.QueueClient.basic_args
documentation:
Call this to add specific AMQP arguments to your argparse parser.
Uses internal argparse object by default. Should be used with setup_from_args()
See QueueBase for details.

```
param parser

argparse object

returns

Modified parser
```

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_client.QueueClient.setup_from_args
documentation:
Converts argparse resulting namespace to ConnectionParameters.
See BaseQueue for details

```
param args

args from your argparse parser extended by basic_args()

return

Used args
```

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_client.QueueClient.setup
documentation:
Sets things up. Takes all argument QueueBase.setup() accepts and few additional

```
param routing_key

Routing key. Uses queue name as routing key if not specified

param exchange

RabbitMQ exchange name. Uses default exchange if not specified

param priority

Messages priority for sending
```

All the rest params are the same as QueueBase.setup(). If used without url - no
basic parameters touched, only client-specific are set

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_client.QueueClient.send
documentation:
Sends an message

```
param body

Message body - string/dict/list. dicts and lists gets packed json

param content_type

Message content_type. This will be set automatically if list or dict supplied

param headers

dict of message headers
```

\:param content_encoding:Message content encoding

```
raises

anything pika.channel.basic_publish() can raise + anything json.dumps() in case of list/dict body
```
This is AMQP client class. It can be used stand-alone, but better use QueueRPC from queue_rpc.py

classes.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.__init__
documentation:
The process initialization.
\:param connection: connection class reference
\:type connection: pika.SelectConnection class, not instance of
\:param params: connection parameters
\:type params: pika.URLParams
\:param prefetch_count: how many messages to prefetch
\:type prefetch_count: int
\:param deads_disabled: disable deads queues routing
\:type deads_disabled: boolean
\:param declare: tristate queue declare parameter ('yes', 'no', 'only')
\:type declare: str
\:param ipc_q_out: python queue for messages and exceptions
\:type ipc_q_out: multiprocessing.JoinableQueue
\:param ipc_q_in: python queue for results
\:type ipc_q_in: multiprocessing.JoinableQueue

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.connect
documentation: Connect command
### BEG: CONNECT-RELATED CALLS AND CALLBACKS

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.on_connection_open
documentation:
Callback for successful connection open
\:param connection: connection just opened
\:type connection: pika.SelectConnection

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess._put_out_exception
documentation:
This is workaround for bug in transer exception instance via IPC queue.
The bug is that \_ipc_q.get() tries to call a constructor (\_\_init\_\_) unexpectedly
with no arguments. This is illegal and
\:param err: error to log in parent
\:type err: Exception

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.on_connection_open_error
documentation:
Callback for connection open error
Destroy ourself if it is
\:param connection: connection failed to open
\:type connection: pika.SelectConnection
\:param err: error description
\:type err: Exception

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.on_channel_open
documentation:
Callback for successful channel opening
\:param channel: channel opened
\:type channel: pika.Channel

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.on_deads_exchange_declared
documentation:
Callback for successful deads exchange declaration
\:param frame: result frame from pika, unused
\:type frame: pika.Frame

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.on_deads_queue_declared
documentation:
Callback for successful deads queue declaration
\:param frame: result frame from pika, unused
\:type frame: pika.Frame

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.on_deads_queue_bind
documentation:
Callback for successful deads queue bind
May be called if 'deads' are disabled
\:param frame: result frame from pika, unused
\:type frame: pika.Frame

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.on_main_queue_declared
documentation:
Callback for successful queue declaration.
May be called if queue is not to be declared'
\:param frame: result frame from pika, unused
\:type frame: pika.Frame

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.on_prefetch_set_ok
documentation:
Callback for basic_qos with prefetch count
\:param frame: result frame from pika, unused
\:type frame: pika.Frame

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.disconnect
documentation: Disconnecting from pika if not done yet
### END: CONNECT-RELATED CALLS AND CALLBACKS
### BEG: DISCONNECT-RELATED CALLS AND CALLBACKS

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.on_consuming_cancel
documentation:
Callback for consuming cancel from our side
\:param frame: result frame from pika, unused
\:type frame: pika.Frame

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.on_connection_closed
documentation:
Callback on connection has been closed event.
\:param connection: connection which has been closed.
\:type connection: pika.SelectConnection
\:param reason: representing reason for loss of connection.
\:type reason: Exception

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.on_channel_closed
documentation:
Callback for channel closed event.
\:param channel: channel just closed
\:type channel: pika.Channel
\:param reason: close reason
\:type reason: Exception

documentation: Main run loop

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess._set_ipc_delay
documentation:
Check if we may reduce \_ipc_q check interval
\:param msg: message process result
\:type msg: IpcMessageResult

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess._increase_ipc_delay
documentation: Increase _ipc_delay to reduce CPU usage when queue is emty for a long time

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.ipc_queue_process
documentation: Callback for interprocess queue checking

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.on_message
documentation:
Callback for recieved message. Re-deliver message to the main worker via \_ipc_q
\:param channel: channel via message was recieved from RabbitMQ, unused
\:type channel: pika.Channel
\:param method: delivery method with exchange, routing key, delivery tag and a redelivered flag
\:type method: pika.Spec.Basic.Deliver
\:param properties: message properties
\:type properties: pika.Spec.BasicProperties
\:param body: message body
\:type body: bytes

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess.report_msg_result
documentation:
Method called to ack/nack message
\:param rslt: result of message process (with message itself)
\:type rslt: IpcMessageResult

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess._stop
documentation:
Calling if the process is to be killed with all its resources
Do not call it in main loop

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess._close_all_ipc_q
documentation:
Close all ipc queues before terminating.
This method is to be called in case of object destroying

class_variables.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_connection_prcs.QueueConnectionProcess._ipc_delay
documentation: pause between interporcess inbout queue checks, in seconds, float

Process-based class to hold RabbitMQ connection.
Should be started as separate process-like thread.

\:param args: arguments parsing result from argparse.ArgumentParser.parse_args()
\:type args: agparse.

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_application.QueueApplication.setup
documentation:
Sets things up.
\:param reconnect: do the reconnect if connection fails
\:type reconnect: boolean

All other params are the same as in parent classes

methods.name: oc_cdt_queue2.queue_application.QueueApplication.main
documentation:
Run this as your application's main() function

```
param cmdline

Command line parameters array for debug purposes

returns

return code for os.exit. Quit your application with this code
```

This class is intended for creating queue consumer application from scratch.
It takes everything needed including command line arguments processing except actual queue processing
that must be done by your own methods supplied by subclassing this class
